name: Santander – city tour circular along the southern coast to the Palacio de la Magdalena
name_original: Santander – city tour circular por la costa sur hasta el Palacio de la Magdalena
region_intro: Waterfall hiking trails around Costera traverse a landscape characterized by diverse ecosystems, including dense forests, coastal paths, and volcanic terrains. The region is known for its abundant rivers and impressive waterfalls, which are often the focal point of many trails. Hikers can expect varied elevations and lush vegetation throughout these routes. Best waterfall hiking trails around Costera The most popular waterfall hiking route is Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas, a 5.2 mile (8.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 21 minutes to complete. This circular route offers views of the Lamiña Waterfalls. Another top favourite among local hikers is Arroyo de la Llana River Trail, an easy 2.8 mile (4.4 km) path. This trail follows the Arroyo de la Llana River, providing a gentle walk through natural scenery. Local hikers also love the Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular by the Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14), a 9.2 mile (14.8 km) trail leading through the natural park, often completed in about 4 hours 17 minutes. Hiking around Costera is defined by dense forests, river valleys, and varied elevations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Costera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 3400 reviews. More than 24000 hikers have used komoot to explore Costera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many waterfall hiking trails can I explore in Costera?
A: Costera offers a diverse selection of 15 dedicated waterfall hiking trails. These routes vary in length and difficulty, providing options for different preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels for waterfall hikes in Costera?
A: The waterfall hikes in Costera cater to a range of abilities. You'll find 4 easy trails, perfect for a relaxed stroll, 7 moderate routes that offer a good challenge, and 4 difficult trails for experienced hikers seeking a more strenuous adventure. The region's varied terrain, from dense forests to river valleys, contributes to this diversity.

Q: What makes the waterfall hiking experience in Costera unique?
A: Waterfall hiking in Costera is unique due to the region's incredibly diverse natural landscape. You'll traverse lush cloud forests, volcanic terrains, and dense deciduous forests, often encountering impressive waterfalls like Nauyaca, La Fortuna, and Rio Celeste. The trails frequently follow rivers and offer opportunities to spot abundant wildlife, including various bird species and monkeys, making each hike a rich sensory experience.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the waterfall trails in Costera?
A: The komoot community highly rates the waterfall trails in Costera, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 3,400 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning natural beauty, the refreshing presence of the waterfalls, and the varied terrain that keeps the routes interesting. Many appreciate the well-maintained paths and the chance to immerse themselves in the region's rich biodiversity.

Q: Are there any family-friendly waterfall hikes in Costera?
A: Yes, Costera has several family-friendly options. For an easy and enjoyable walk suitable for most ages, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996713975/arroyo-de-la-llana-river-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arroyo de la Llana River Trail&lt;/a&gt;. This 2.8-mile (4.4 km) path follows the river, offering gentle terrain and beautiful natural scenery without significant elevation changes, making it ideal for families.

Q: Can I find circular waterfall routes in Costera?
A: Absolutely. Circular routes are a great way to experience the scenery without retracing your steps. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996739843/lamina-waterfalls-circular-from-barcenillas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lamiña Waterfalls - circular from Barcenillas&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.2-mile (8.3 km) trail that showcases the beautiful Lamiña Waterfalls. Another excellent choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e996716522/parque-natural-de-los-collados-del-ason-circular-by-the-camino-del-val-del-ason-pr-s-14" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de los Collados del Asón – circular by the Camino del Val del Asón (PR-S 14)&lt;/a&gt;, a longer 9.2-mile (14.8 km) route through a stunning natural park.

Q: What is the best time of year to visit the waterfalls in Costera?
A: The best time to visit waterfalls in Costera is generally during the rainy season, which typically runs from May to November. While you might encounter more rain, this is when the waterfalls are at their most powerful and impressive, fed by abundant rainfall. The surrounding vegetation is also incredibly lush and vibrant during this period. However, trails can be muddier, so appropriate footwear is essential.

Q: Are there any interesting historical sites or landmarks near the waterfall trails in Costera?
A: Yes, the Costera region is rich in history and natural beauty beyond the waterfalls. You can explore charming historical towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/360503" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santillana del Mar Historic Town Centre&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/344751" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic center of Comillas&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in architecture, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3828681" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magdalena Palace&lt;/a&gt; and various castles such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6006740" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sobrellano Palace&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6656175" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa Ana Castle and Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt; are also nearby. These attractions offer a great complement to a day of hiking.

Q: What should I wear for waterfall hikes in Costera?
A: Given the region's diverse ecosystems and potential for rain, it's advisable to wear moisture-wicking clothing, sturdy waterproof hiking boots with good grip, and bring a lightweight rain jacket. Trails can be muddy, especially during the rainy season, and river crossings might occur. Sun protection like a hat and sunscreen is also recommended, as is insect repellent.

Q: Are there opportunities for wild swimming near the waterfalls in Costera?
A: Many waterfalls in Costera, such as La Fortuna and Nauyaca, feature natural pools at their base that are popular for swimming. However, it's crucial to check local regulations and safety warnings for each specific waterfall. Always prioritize safety, be aware of strong currents, and only swim in designated or clearly safe areas.

Q: Can I bring my dog on waterfall hikes in Costera?
A: While many outdoor areas in Costera are dog-friendly, policies can vary significantly for specific trails, national parks, or private waterfall attractions. It's always best to check the individual trail or park regulations beforehand. Look for signage at trailheads or consult official park websites to confirm if dogs are permitted and if they need to be on a leash.

Q: Are there any hidden gem waterfall hikes in Costera that are less crowded?
A: While specific 'hidden gems' can change with popularity, exploring some of the lesser-known trails or visiting during off-peak hours can lead to a more secluded experience. Consider trails that are slightly longer or have a moderate difficulty rating, as these often deter larger crowds. Researching local guides or forums might also reveal less-trafficked spots that offer stunning, tranquil waterfall views.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on waterfall hikes in Costera?
A: Costera's rich biodiversity means you could encounter a wide array of wildlife. Along the trails, keep an eye out for various bird species, including neo-tropical songbirds, and listen for their calls. You might also spot howler monkeys, iguanas, and butterflies. In some areas, unique flora and fauna like the white-nosed coati can be observed, especially in higher elevations or protected reserves.
tips:
The Cathedral of Our Lady of the Assumption (Santander) is located very close to the sea and its appearance is very austere if we compare it with others of the time.

The history of the cathedral dates back to the times when Santander was granted the status of jurisdiction in 1187, under the tutelage of the abbot of its church, this meant a great economic boost for the town with which the cathedral could be built and the castle, which was later called Puebla Vieja, as well as walls that defended the busy port and its inhabitants.

The current building of the Cathedral of Santander is made up of 2 overlapping Gothic-style churches. The lower one was built in the first third of the s. XIII is the parish of Christ and the upper one was built during that century and has had to be rebuilt and enlarged, in 1941 Santander suffered a fire that affected this part of the Cathedral.
This small but representative building is part of the port's landing stage.
The very famous sculpture of Los Raqueros.

The entire Magdalena Peninsula is well worth the visit, because it seems like a separate point within the city, but if you are going to visit the peninsula, take into account this small lighthouse, which is so close but passes many sometimes unnoticed and has a beautiful profile perched on that steep rock.
The Palacio de la Magdalena is owned by the Spanish royal family. It was built in the early 1900s and is one of the most emblematic and visited buildings in Santander.

Small Mini Zoo within the enclosure of the Peninsula de la Magdalena, next to the Muelle de las Carabelas and it is easily accessible as it is very close to the entrance if you take the path to the left.
In this small zoo you will find seals, sea lions and penguins, the enclosures can be found with more or less water depending on the tides: sometimes you find them very empty with the seals barely swimming in a puddle and other times it is completely full of Water.
In fact, if you know a little about the times of their meals, you can see how the keepers feed them.
Surfer's beach with islands on the horizon

Walk along the seafront of Santander from the Chiqui viewpoint to the Hotel Real passing through the Sardinero beaches and the Magdalena peninsula
Parking at the Sardinero Stadium and returning inside
The Palacio de Deportes de Santander is an arena in Santander for basketball and handball.

A park located between Honduras and Avenida de los Castros, at the height of the University of Sciences. It has a Skateboard track, children's area, dog walking area, benches to rest and fountains. The most notable are the escalators that avoid the slope and facilitate the climb.
It is the most used park for university parties and punctual drinks (when they can be done).

Church of the early seventeenth century of Mannerist style following the model of the Roman church of II Gesú de Vignola. It is believed to be a work by Juan de Nates.
It was completely restored in the 40s of the last century because it was very affected by the great fire of Santander in 1941, in fact its west wing is completely redone since nothing remained of it.
